codeReview.summary: Root cause correctly diagnosed and fixed. The dirty rect in render_content() only cleared from the previous cursor row downward (TEXT_Y + prev_cursor_y), but draw_tt() re-renders ALL text from the top of the text area. Lines above the cursor were never cleared between frames, causing alpha blending to accumulate — each keystroke on a lower line re-blended text on upper lines, producing progressively thicker/bolder strokes. The fix replaces the dynamic clear_start_y with the constant TEXT_Y, ensuring the entire text content region is cleared before every re-render. This is a correct, minimal fix that addresses the root cause rather than papering over the symptom. The commit message clearly documents the root cause and rationale. Visual verification confirmed identical stroke weight across all lines before and after cursor movement. Five subsequent commits (double-buffering, damage tracking, oversampling, resolution config, library docs) built cleanly on top of this fix.

issues.description: PREV_CURSOR_Y is still written to (line 279 at time of commit) but never read after this fix. It's a dead store. The worker acknowledged this in discoveredIssues. Later commits changed it from u32 to [u32; 2] for double-buffering, perpetuating the dead write. Should be cleaned up when damage tracking is revisited — it's harmless but could confuse future readers into thinking it affects clearing logic.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rendering invariant 'every line must be cleared before re-rendering to avoid alpha accumulation from draw_coverage blending' is not documented anywhere (not in AGENTS.md, architecture.md, or code comments beyond the fix itself). This is the kind of subtle invariant that could be re-broken by future dirty-rect optimizations that try to minimize the clear region for performance. A note in the architecture doc's Drawing Library or Display Pipeline section would help prevent regression.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The bug was caused by an optimization (partial clear from cursor row) that violated this invariant. The fix comment in compositor/main.rs explains the local rationale but the architectural principle isn't captured in .factory/library/architecture.md.

summary: PASS. The worker correctly diagnosed hypothesis #1 (accumulated alpha blending from partial clears) as the root cause via code analysis and QEMU visual verification (before/after screenshots). The fix is minimal and correct: replacing the dynamic clear_start_y (which started at the cursor row) with TEXT_Y (top of text area), ensuring the full text region is cleared before draw_tt() re-renders all lines. All 675 tests passed. Visual verification confirmed consistent stroke weight across all lines regardless of cursor position. One non-blocking issue: dead PREV_CURSOR_Y write. One shared-state observation: the rendering invariant should be documented in the architecture library to prevent future regression.
